guide:boards:
# Whiteboard — usage guide (read-only)

The pane renders a JSON document under context key `boards`:
  { "active"?: <board id>, "boards": [ Board, ... ] }

Each Board: { id, title, type, comments?: [{by,ts,text}], ...type fields }.
Types and their fields:
  - mermaid: { source }              — a mermaid diagram (flowchart/sequence/ER/…), rendered strict.
  - table:   { columns[], rows[][], align?[] }  — an editable grid. align ∈ left|right|center.
  - chart:   { chart, x:{label,categories[]}, y:{label}, series:[{name, values[] | points[[x,y]]}] }
               chart ∈ bar | line | area | scatter | pie. scatter/pie: pie uses series[0].values
               against x.categories; scatter uses series[].points (numeric x,y, no categories).
  - note:    { markdown }            — a markdown note.

## How to write
- PREFER `edit_whiteboard__boards` (targeted find-and-replace) for small changes: a single cell,
  a comment reply, one node label, one series value. It leaves the rest of the doc byte-for-byte,
  so user edits and comments elsewhere never drift.
- Use `set_whiteboard__boards` only for a deliberate restructure. Keep the JSON valid and
  pretty-printed; append new boards at the END of the array so diffs stay small.
- NEVER drop a board or a comment you did not deliberately change. The user edits cells, diagram
  source, and adds comments in the pane; those arrive back to you in this same document. Preserve
  them. Unknown fields you don't recognize must also be preserved.

## Comments are the talk-back channel
Every board carries `comments[]` ({ by: "user"|"agent", ts, text }). The user replies to your
boards there. Respond to a comment either in chat OR by appending an agent comment on that board
(by:"agent") and revising the board — do not delete the user's comment.

## Steering attention
Set top-level `active` to a board id to focus the pane on it. The pane honors your `active` unless
the user clicked a tab within the last ~30s (their manual choice wins briefly).

## Keep it small
The doc is capped near maxTokens×4 chars. Keep tables compact; split a large table into two
boards rather than one giant grid. The pane warns the user when the doc nears the budget.
